steps.title: Identify the logical trigger in the 'too...to' construction
steps.content:
The key signal is the phrase “economic issues cannot remain the domain only of experts” combined with “too ______ to leave to economists alone.” This structure means: because economics has a certain property, it cannot be confined to experts—the public must be involved.

What property would make something too important for experts to monopolize?

steps.title: Match the meaning to the choices
steps.content:
Critical (essential/crucial to a functioning democracy) fits the logic: if economics is too important, the public must understand it and experts cannot have sole control.

The other three fail: complex would suggest experts should handle it more, while respectable and accessible don't explain why public involvement is necessary.

That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
